steps.title: Spot the defining contrast
steps.content: The passage establishes one method for cartographers (starting from the lowest point), then pivots to hydrologists' opposite method (starting from the top). This reversal is the defining feature.

steps.title: Match the contrast signal
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is contrast. Cartographers start at the lowest point (the Yukon River); hydrologists start at the top (the Llewellyn Glacier). The blank needs a transition signaling that the second method is the opposite of the first.
  • Choice A — "In a similar way,": signals that the second sentence parallels the first. But hydrologists begin at the top while cartographers begin at the bottom — these are opposite, not similar, approaches. ✗
  • Choice B — "By contrast,": signals direct opposition between the two methods, fitting the top-vs.-bottom structure perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — "For example,": signals that the second sentence is a specific instance of a general claim. Hydrologists are not an example of what cartographers do — they are an opposing case. ✗
  • Choice D — "In other words,": signals that the second sentence restates the first in different terms. The two sentences describe different methods, not the same method rephrased. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
